ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken breast entry; no specific nugget/roasted preparation code exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked chicken meat broadly; no nugget-specific roasted preparation code exists in the published hierarchy. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for roasted chicken breast nuggets; left null to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 5 roasted chicken breast nuggets, weighing approximately 120 g, typically consumed as a main protein component at lunch or dinner.

nutrient_source.title: Roasted Chicken Breast 5Pc Nuggets — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Roasted Chicken Breast 5Pc Nuggets (cr_id G00010423). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on breaded/coated chicken products and high-protein low-carbohydrate meat dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for roasted chicken breast nuggets in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous breaded/coated chicken products and high-protein, low-carbohydrate meat preparations documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Carbohydrate content is 7.34 g/100g; serving size 120 g yields 8.8 g available carbohydrate. GL = (45 × 8.8) / 100 = 3.96, rounded to 4.0 per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 454 mg/100g — exceeds the 400 mg/100g threshold for hypertension suitability
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 80 g per meal and request reduced soy sauce in the marinade, as the 454 mg sodium per 100 g significantly contributes to daily sodium load; avoid additional dipping sauces to prevent further sodium accumulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (7.34 g/100g) and high protein (21.15 g/100g) support glycaemic stability, but sugar content (5.98 g/100g) from marinade sweeteners warrants monitoring
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roasted chicken breast nuggets are generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes given the low carbohydrate and high protein profile; however, the 5.98 g sugar per 100 g from honey or sugar in the marinade should be accounted for in daily carbohydrate budgeting, and sweet dipping sauces must be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (454 mg/100g) and moderate fat (9.15 g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~2.5 g/100g; roasting method avoids excess trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high sodium content (454 mg/100g) is the primary concern and portion should be limited to 80–100 g; the roasting preparation method is preferable to deep-frying as it avoids trans fat formation, and the estimated saturated fat of approximately 2.5 g/100g remains within acceptable limits for a single serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory marinade ingredients (garlic, ginger) are beneficial; elevated sodium may worsen tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The garlic and ginger in the marinade provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols (allicin, gingerols) that may benefit rheumatoid arthritis management; however, sodium at 454 mg/100g may exacerbate RA-related tissue swelling and reduced-sodium preparation is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted Chicken Breast Nuggets (5-piece) is a Singapore food-service dish consisting of boneless chicken breast pieces marinated in a soy sauce, garlic, ginger, and honey or sugar blend, then coated lightly in breadcrumbs and oven-roasted or baked until golden. The result is a tender, juicy interior with a lightly crisp exterior and a mildly sweet-savoury flavour profile. The dish is commonly served as a protein-forward main at lunch or dinner in Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and casual dining outlets. Nutritionally, it provides 202 kcal, 21.15 g protein, 9.15 g fat, and 7.34 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making it a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate option. Sodium is elevated at 454 mg/100g due to soy sauce and salt in the marinade, which is the primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The low carbohydrate and high protein content make it broadly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when consumed without sweet dipping sauces. The roasting preparation method avoids the excess saturated and trans fats associated with deep-frying, and the anti-inflammatory marinade ingredients (garlic, ginger) may offer additional benefit for inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 5 chicken nuggets?
Roasted chicken breast nuggets nutrition Singapore
Is chicken nuggets suitable for diabetics?
Baked chicken nuggets vs fried chicken nuggets

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010423, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved regional source for Singapore food records. The exact dish entry was found in the HPB database with full macro and key micronutrient coverage.
notes: saturated_fat_g value of 2.5 is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for roasted chicken breast with light breading (USDA SR Legacy item 05064 and related entries) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry; this is documented as an adapted value. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for Roasted Chicken Breast 5Pc Nuggets as of 2026-04-16. GI value of 45 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for breaded/coated chicken products and high-protein low-carbohydrate meat preparations; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore food-court chicken preparation standards; formal halal certification should be verified with the specific vendor. The dish is classified as Western cuisine in the HPB source and the input record; it is sold in Singapore food-service contexts and therefore multilingual naming for Singaporean dishes (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) has been applied per G6 rules.
